core_mechanism: parametric (dispersive) coupling between two resonant modes in which one oscillator's displacement modulates the resonant frequency of the other, operated at a symmetric point so the frequency shift depends on the square of the displacement — yielding a measurement coupled to the oscillator's invariant (energy/amplitude) rather than its instantaneous position
brief_reason: The abstract describes a cavity whose resonance is detuned by the motion of a membrane placed between fixed mirrors, giving direct measurement of the square of the displacement and hence access to the oscillator's energy eigenstate rather than its position.
